data.description:
## Why
`_acquire_fcntl` catches every `OSError` identically (`src/rebar/_store/lock.py:127-140`):
it waits and ultimately returns the timeout sentinel `-1`, so EINTR/ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF are
indistinguishable from real EAGAIN/EACCES contention and get masked as a spurious 30-60s
`LockTimeout`. The mkdir leg already branches on `errno` (`:222-230`). VALIDATED: no direct
test of `_acquire_fcntl`.

## What
Branch on `errno` — retry-until-deadline for contention (EAGAIN/EACCES), retry-without-
burning-budget for EINTR, and surface other errors immediately with their identity — and
add direct tests.

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` `_acquire_fcntl`; new direct tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] EAGAIN/EACCES: retry until deadline (unchanged contention behavior).
- [ ] EINTR: retried without treating it as contention-budget exhaustion.
- [ ] Other errors (ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF): surfaced immediately with original errno.
- [ ] The fd is closed on every failure path.
- [ ] `make test` green.
data.title: Discriminate contention from unexpected errors in _acquire_fcntl
